text: Artificial intelligence is increasingly common in the modern workplace. Many companies use automation to handle repetitive tasks, such as data entry or customer service queries, which allows human workers to focus on more complex and creative responsibilities. Productivity tools powered by AI also help employees organize their schedules, analyze large amounts of information, and make better decisions. However, successful integration often requires human-AI collaboration, where people and machines work together, combining human judgment with AI’s speed and accuracy. This shift is changing job roles, requiring new skills like data analysis and digital literacy. While some jobs may disappear, others will evolve or be created, emphasizing the need for lifelong learning and adaptability.
